target_audience.details: PRIMARY NOMINATORS: Professional truck drivers — skewed male 35–65+. Experienced enough to have a mental list of the best drivers they know. Pride and peer respect are the emotional levers, not money. ACQUISITION TARGETS: The nominated drivers — warm-market leads, pre-sold by a trusted peer before LHS ever contacts them. CULTURAL SEEDING AUDIENCE: Broader driver community on Facebook (primary), Instagram, TikTok. Mobile-first mandatory. The older male segment is less comfortable with social tagging behavior — all CTAs must frame participation as status and respect, not social media mechanics. KEY INSIGHT: The audience does NOT need to be sold on trucking pride. They already have it. They need a credible, easy mechanism to express it. The nomination mechanic provides that mechanism.

campaign_goal.primary_goal: Build a viral driver referral network by converting engaged drivers into peer nominators. The public message is: nominate the best drivers you know. The referral math happens behind the scenes. Every nomination generates shareable content automatically, creating an exponential social content engine alongside the membership growth engine.

key_messages: Every driver has a type — Pit Stop King, Night Owl, Road Scholar — and your archetype deserves recognition. Tag the driver you know like this.
We're building the best driver network in the country. If you know a great driver, nominate them. That's it.
You've been nominated for the Top 10 Truckers Club by [driver name]. Here's your invitation.

cta.text: Nominate your Top 10. Or start with one — tag the best driver you know.
cta.lead_capture_point: THREE-STAGE FUNNEL: (1) PUBLIC — social content drives a single CTA: 'Tag ONE driver.' No program explanation needed. (2) LANDER — authenticated nomination form. Driver enters their info + up to 10 nominee names/contacts. Looks and feels like a nomination form, not a referral signup. (3) LULU FLOW — post-authentication conversational flow. Lulu activates the driver's nomination queue, generates shareable nomination cards, creates driver portal. URL: TBD — new lander required. Target launch: 2 weeks from 2026-03-10.

budget.notes: Episodes 1–3 complete (Pit Stop King, Night Owl, Road Scholar — all in 16x9, 9x16 reel, and static formats). 7 archetype episodes remaining — production credits needed. Draft Day assets complete but performing below expectations on engagement. Budget for paid retargeting TBD once nomination lander is live. CEO video (Jeff Swenson, under 90 seconds) needed before launch.

creative_assets.title: Episode 1 — Pit Stop King (16x9 + 9x16 reel + static)
creative_assets.notes: COMPLETED. All three formats produced. In use for Phase 1 retargeting.

creative_assets.title: Draft Day Series — Spoof Episodes
creative_assets.notes: COMPLETED. PERFORMANCE NOTE: Draft Day is not catching traction. Low tags, low reactions. Confirmed by G-Man as underperforming. Root cause: entertainment without a participation mechanic. The audience watches but has no clear single action to take. RESOLUTION: Do not abandon Draft Day assets — convert them into a BRIDGE to the nomination program (see Draft Day Bridge initiative).

creative_assets.title: Top 10 Truckers White Paper — Strategic Framework
creative_assets.notes: Existing strategic doc. Source of record for referral program structure.

creative_assets.title: Nomination Card Templates — Auto-Generated by Lulu
creative_assets.notes: NEEDED. Template-based shareable graphics. Format: '[Driver name] nominated [Nominee name] for the Top 10 Truckers Club.' With Outrider avatar, Top 10 Truckers logo, truck background, CTA. One card generated per nomination automatically. This is the viral content engine — if 1,000 drivers nominate 10 each, 10,000 cards are auto-created. Driver can share it themselves or Lulu sends it on their behalf.

creative_assets.notes: NEEDED. Under 90 seconds. No math, no pyramid, no diagram. Script direction: 'Every great driver knows other great drivers. So we built something simple. A national network of the best drivers on the road. If you know great drivers, nominate them.' Authentic, direct, no teleprompter energy. G-Man confirms Jeff should appear as the builder/advocate, not the salesperson.

creative_assets.title: Remaining 7 Archetype Videos (Episodes 4–10)
creative_assets.notes: NEEDED. See archetype_backlog for full list. Each episode follows the same format as Episodes 1–3: 16x9 + 9x16 reel + static. Each ends with: 'Know a driver like this? Nominate them.' Production credits needed.

creative_assets.title: SMS Copy Variants — Lulu Outbound Referral Messaging
creative_assets.notes: NEEDED. Lulu-generated. Personalized per nomination. Driver can send or Lulu sends on their behalf. Must feel like it came from the nominating driver, not from LHS corporate.

plan.strategic_context: Top Ten Truckers is a FUNNEL play, not a content play. The archetype videos are top-of-funnel identity hooks — not the campaign itself. The campaign is the referral engine underneath. The critical strategic update from this session: the public message must never lead with the referral structure. The referral math (10 → 100 → 1,000) is real and powerful, but it triggers MLM/pyramid associations the moment you explain it. The fix is simple: never lead with math, always lead with club psychology. Publicly, this campaign is about nominating the best drivers you know. Internally, every nomination feeds the referral engine. The Draft Day content series has confirmed that entertainment without participation does not generate tags. The campaign fix is separating content into three tiers — Entertainment (builds audience, ends with single tag ask), Nomination (directly asks for one tag), and Program Explanation (only after interest is shown). The two-week launch target requires: a new nomination lander, Lulu authentication flow, and nomination card auto-generation. These are the minimum viable pieces before any paid campaign can drive meaningful conversion.

organic_plan_90_day.weekly_focus_notes: Phase 1 (Weeks 1–2): LAUNCH PREP. Build nomination lander + Lulu authentication flow + nomination card template. Produce CEO video. Produce Draft Day Bridge video. Minimum 5 pieces of content ready before launch. Phase 2 (Weeks 3–6): LAUNCH. Nomination lander goes live. Draft Day Bridge video releases. Begin tagging-mechanic posts with single CTA: 'Tag ONE driver.' CEO video releases Week 3 or 4. Continue archetype series — Episodes 4 and 5 drop. Phase 3 (Weeks 7–12): SCALE. Nomination volume drives auto-generated content. Driver portals activate showing nomination status and network size. Remaining archetype episodes (6–10) release weekly. Nomination spotlight posts amplify nominees. Lulu outbound referral flow operating.

initiatives.title: Nomination Lander — New Build (2-Week Target)
next_actions: Design lander — look and feel: a nomination form, NOT a referral signup. Identity: Top 10 Truckers Club.
Headline: 'Nominate the Best Drivers on the Road.'
Subheadline: 'The Top 10 Truckers Club is building the best driver network in the country. If you know great drivers, nominate them.'
Form structure: Driver info section (name, phone, email, optional member number) + Nominee section (up to 10 names with phone OR email per nominee)
CTA button: 'Submit My Nominations'
Post-submit: Driver is authenticated and handed to Lulu. Lulu activates their nomination queue.
Authentication required — must prevent non-member spam. Members authenticate via member number or phone match.
initiatives.notes: The form must feel like a nomination form, not a referral form. The emotional framing is: 'I am recognizing great drivers I know.' Not: 'I am signing up for a referral program.' The LHS brand appears but this page is primarily Top 10 Truckers Club branded. Summer 2026 national network launch is the urgency hook — 'Top 10 Truckers Club going live this summer.'

initiatives.title: Lulu Top 10 Authentication + Nomination Flow
next_actions: Lulu activation message post-lander submission: 'Hey, this is Lulu from LineHaul Station. You're now part of the Top 10 Truckers program. Who do you want to nominate?'
Driver can respond two ways: (A) paste name + phone/email directly into Lulu chat, or (B) click link to nomination form
Lulu creates a task queue entry per nomination: Driver name, Nominee name, contact info, status: Pending nomination content
Lulu generates nomination card for each submission
Lulu confirms back to driver: 'Sounds good. I'll let you know when I'm ready to contact [nominee] — we're making something cool for them to see.'
Driver portal is created showing: nominations submitted, driver joined, network size
Lulu sends nomination outreach to nominees: 'You were nominated for the Top 10 Truckers Club by [driver name].' With nomination card and link.
initiatives.notes: The conversational Lulu flow removes friction. Driver does not need to navigate an app. They just tell Lulu who they want to nominate. Name + phone or email is the minimum viable input. The driver can paste all 10 at once or send one at a time. Lulu handles the queue. Anti-spam: authentication must be confirmed before Lulu activates the nomination flow. No unauthenticated user can submit nominations.

initiatives.title: Nomination Card Auto-Generation Engine
next_actions: Design base nomination card template: [Driver name] nominated [Nominee name] for the Top 10 Truckers Club
Template elements: Outrider avatar of nominating driver, Top 10 Truckers logo, truck background, CTA (Join the club — [URL])
Cards generated automatically per nomination — no manual production
Distribution options: (A) System sends the card to the nominee via email/SMS with message from driver, OR (B) Card sent to driver for them to share personally
Nominee-facing version: 'You've been nominated as a Top 10 Trucker by [name].' With card visual.
Nominator-facing version: '[Nominee name] has been added to your Top 10. We're reaching out to them soon.' With shareable card driver can post themselves.
initiatives.notes: This is the viral content engine. If 1,000 authenticated drivers nominate 10 each, 10,000 nomination cards are auto-generated. Each card is a piece of shareable social proof. Drivers will post their own nomination card because it makes them look good to their network. The nominee will post their card because being nominated is a status signal. This generates exponential social content at zero incremental production cost.

initiatives.title: Draft Day Bridge — Convert to Nomination Funnel
next_actions: Produce one Draft Day Bridge video: Draft Day episode closes with — 'Today's Draft Day is brought to you by the Top 10 Truckers program at LineHaul Station. Top 10 Truckers Club going live this summer as the national network launches. Nominate your first-round picks at [URL].'
This is a bridge, not an abandonment. Draft Day content stays in market but now has a destination.
All future Draft Day content ends with a nomination CTA
Existing Draft Day videos: add nomination CTA in caption if possible without recutting
initiatives.notes: CONFIRMED PERFORMANCE ISSUE: Draft Day has generated few tags, few reactions, few shares. G-Man assessment: it is not catching traction. Root diagnosis: entertainment without a participation mechanic. Drivers watch but have no clear single action. The bridge strategy converts the existing asset base rather than writing it off. Do NOT scrap existing Draft Day content — the audience building it has done (however modest) is still real. The bridge video transitions that audience into the nomination system.

initiatives.title: Archetype Video Series — Episodes 1–10
next_actions: Episodes 1–3 complete and in retargeting (Pit Stop King, Night Owl, Road Scholar)
Update ALL episode captions and CTAs: change from 'Tell Lulu who's on your Top 10' to 'Tag ONE driver like this' — single ask, lower friction
Each episode now ends with nomination CTA once lander is live
Produce Episodes 4–10 per archetype_backlog below — one per week cadence starting at launch
initiatives.notes: The single-ask CTA fix is critical. Current CTA ('Tell Lulu who's on your Top 10') implies a multi-step process. Drivers disengage. The new ask: 'Tag the driver you know like this.' Just one. Tag one friend. Once they tag, the nomination system takes over. If 10,000 drivers tag one friend each, that is 10,000 warm referrals — same outcome as asking for ten.

initiatives.title: Pyramid/MLM Messaging Guard — All Campaign Copy
next_actions: Audit all existing campaign copy for any 10→100→1,000 math language — remove it
Replace all referral math framing with club psychology framing
Approved public framing: 'We're building the best driver network in the country. Every driver in the club invites their Top 10.'

Approved internal explanation (for drivers who ask how the rewards work): 'Every driver who joins can bring others in. You earn terminal access based on your network's usage — the more drivers you bring in, and the more they bring in, the more access you earn. No one at any level is at a disadvantage. The person who joined last has the same ability to build as anyone else.'
NEVER use in public content: pyramid, levels, tiers, 10x10x10, exponential math
initiatives.notes: Jeff's concern is valid and was confirmed in the transcript: the moment someone hears 10 refer 10 refer 10 they think MLM or pyramid. The fix is to never explain the math in public content. The math is a reward structure detail that gets explained after interest is established — not as the campaign hook. Public hook is always: 'Nominate great drivers.' Reward details live in the driver portal and CEO video, not in social posts.

initiatives.title: Tagging Mechanic Posts — Single Ask Engagement
next_actions: Create 5 engagement posts using the single-ask tagging mechanic
Post format 1 — Tag the Legend: 'Every driver knows one legend. The guy who never misses a delivery. The one who can back a trailer blindfolded. Tag that driver.'
Post format 2 — Truck Stop Story: Short video clip, driver says 'You ever meet one of those drivers that can back a 53-footer into a hole the size of a mailbox?' Cut to text: 'Tag that driver.'
Post format 3 — Archetype Poll: 'Which driver do you know? Pit Stop King / Night Owl / Road Scholar / Hammer Down Cowboy. Tag them.'
Post format 4 — Draft Board: Graphic showing Top 10 Truckers Draft Board with blank slots. 'Who's getting drafted? Tag them.'
Post format 5 — Legend Line: 'Every driver knows one legend. Tag yours.' Clean visual. No explanation.
initiatives.notes: These posts exist purely to generate tags. No program explanation. No referral talk. The system takes over after the tag. Paid amplification on these posts is recommended at modest spend — the tagging mechanic makes them naturally shareable.

initiatives.title: Driver Portal — Gamification Layer
next_actions: Driver portal shows: nominations submitted, nominees who joined, network size
Display format: 'Your nominations: 7 | Drivers joined: 3 | Your network: 24'
Portal updates in real time as nominations convert
Driver can also nominate directly from the portal (alternative to Lulu chat flow)
initiatives.notes: Gamification drives repeat engagement. Drivers who can see their network growing will nominate more. The network size number is the key metric to show — it makes the abstract reward structure concrete and visible.

Post-launch KPIs: nomination lander visits, nominations submitted, nomination cards generated, nominee open rate, nominee-to-member conversion rate, driver portal activation count.

nomination_system_spec.overview: Every nomination is a three-output event: (1) a task entry in Lulu's queue, (2) a nomination card graphic auto-generated, (3) outreach to the nominee. The system does not require the driver to take multiple steps — they give Lulu a name and a contact, and the system handles the rest.
driver_flow: Step 1: Driver lands on Top 10 Truckers lander via social content or direct share
Step 2: Driver enters their info + up to 10 nominee names/contacts. Form looks like a nomination form, not a referral signup.
Step 3: Authentication check — member number or phone match required. Non-members flagged for standard Outriders signup first.
Step 4: Lulu activates. 'You're in the Top 10 Truckers program. Who do you want to nominate?' Driver can chat names to Lulu directly OR use the form.
Step 5: Lulu generates a nomination card per nominee. Confirms: 'Got it. We're making something for [nominee name] to see. We'll let you know when we reach out.'
Step 6: Driver portal populates with nomination list and status.
Step 7: Lulu sends outreach to nominee: 'You were nominated for the Top 10 Truckers Club by [driver name].' With nomination card and join link.
nominee_flow: Nominee receives email or SMS: '[Driver name] nominated you for the Top 10 Truckers Club at LineHaul Station.'
Nomination card is attached — visually compelling, personalized
Nominee lands on Top 10 Truckers Club lander
Nominee is now eligible to nominate their own Top 10 — Lulu activates their flow
anti_spam_rules: Authentication required before any nominations can be submitted
Member must be verified (member number or phone match) before Lulu activates nomination flow
Non-members are routed to standard Outriders signup before accessing the nomination system
Nomination volume limits TBD — monitor for abuse once live
nomination_system_spec.reward_structure_note: Reward details (terminal access tied to network usage volume) are NOT explained in public-facing content. They are explained in the CEO video, the driver portal, and in Lulu conversation after a driver is authenticated. Public content only ever says: join the club, nominate great drivers. The reward mechanism is a conversion closer, not the hook.

engagement_plan.weekly_targets: Pre-launch: engage trucking Facebook communities with archetype content — single-ask tag CTAs only. Respond to every tag within 24 hours. Post-launch: engage with nomination cards when drivers share them publicly. Every public share of a nomination card is a touchpoint for LHS account to comment warmly. Watch for organic group shares of archetype content and amplify by engaging in comments.
engagement_plan.notes: Single most important engagement fix: make it easy to tag ONE person, not ten. Every post, every caption, every video end card — the ask is always ONE tag. After they tag, the system takes over. The nomination flow handles the rest. Older male drivers do not tag naturally — the framing must always be respect and recognition, never social media behavior. 'Tag the legend you know' is a compliment to the tagged driver. That framing makes the action feel right to this audience.

flags: DRAFT DAY UNDERPERFORMING — confirmed by G-Man: low tags, low reactions, not catching traction. Root cause: entertainment without participation mechanic. Resolution: Draft Day Bridge video converts existing assets into nomination funnel entry points. Do not abandon — redirect.
NOMINATION LANDER NOT YET BUILT — required before any paid campaign can drive meaningful conversion. This is the #1 build priority. 2-week launch target.
LULU NOMINATION FLOW NOT YET BUILT — Lulu must be activated for Top 10 before launch. Authentication + task queue + card generation.
CEO VIDEO NOT YET PRODUCED — needed at launch. Jeff Swenson, under 90 seconds, no referral math.
EXISTING EPISODE CTAs NEED UPDATE — current CTA on Episodes 1–3 is 'Tell Lulu who's on your Top 10' — multi-step implied, reduces engagement. Update to single-ask: 'Tag the [archetype] you know.'
PYRAMID/MLM LANGUAGE — audit all existing copy for math framing (10→100→1000). Remove from all public content. Reward details move to driver portal and CEO video only.
NOMINATION CARD TEMPLATE NOT DESIGNED — required before Lulu can generate them. Blocking Phase 2.
AUTHENTICATION LOGIC NOT DEFINED — how does a member prove identity on the lander? Member number? Phone match? Must be defined before build.

Produce CEO video — Jeff Swenson, under 90 seconds, no math, authentic tone
Produce Draft Day Bridge video — closes with nomination CTA and summer launch hook
Update captions on Episodes 1–3 to single-ask CTA ('Tag the [archetype] you know.')
Audit all existing campaign copy — remove all 10→100→1000 math language from public-facing content
Soft launch to existing Outriders members first — seed initial nominations before public paid push
Define authentication method for lander (member number or phone match)
Set memberships_per_week_target once nomination flow is live and producing data
notes: This is a FUNNEL play, not a content play. Archetype videos are the top-of-funnel identity hook. The campaign is the nomination engine underneath.
SINGLE-ASK FIX is the most important immediate change. Every piece of content asks for ONE tag. Not ten. The nomination system handles the rest. This is the root fix for Draft Day's low engagement.

NEVER LEAD WITH MATH in public content. The referral structure (10→100→1,000) is real and is how rewards work — but it triggers MLM associations. Public message: nominate great drivers. Reward details live in the portal and CEO video only.

The nomination card auto-generation is the viral engine. Every nomination creates shareable content at zero incremental cost. Protect this mechanic — it is what makes this campaign potentially exponential.

Drivers love recognition more than money. 'You've been nominated as a Top 10 Trucker' is a status signal. That is why nominees will open the outreach, share the card, and join. Design everything around that emotional core.

Soft launch to existing Outriders members first before any paid push. Seed 100–200 nominations organically before the lander goes into paid rotation. The first nomination cards going out to nominees are the proof that the system works — get the flow right in a controlled environment first.
Old School Legend (T10-EP-010) connects directly to CMP-0008 Year of the Trucker nostalgia angle. Consider coordinated cross-post when that episode releases.
G-Man = Jeff McCuskey. Jeff = Jeff Swenson. Convention maintained throughout this record.
